what: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: Computes approximate "total coverage" metric combining: 1) line coverage percentage (`percent_cov`, 0–100), 2) conservative proxy breadth tests based counts passing    test files, functions without tests, skipped test files. final metric scales line coverage (proportion) fraction tested total function-space, : - `n_testfiles` = number passing test files (0, treated 1 avoid divide--zero), - `n_no_function_tests` = number functions lacking tests (`nrow(functions_no_tests_df)`), - `n_skipped_files` = number skipped test files (`length(tests_skipped)`), - `total_functions` = `n_tested + n_untested` `n_tested = max(n_testfiles, 1)`, - `total_cov` = (percent_cov / 100) * (n_tested / total_functions).

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: named list : total_cov (numeric): Approximate total coverage (proportion 0–1). percent_cov_round (numeric): total_cov rounded 2 decimals (still 0–1). n_testfiles (integer): Count passing test files. n_no_function_tests (integer): Count functions lacking tests. n_skipped_files (integer): Count skipped test files.

title: Compute approximate total coverage combining line coverage and test breadth — compute_total_coverage
text: function intentionally conservative: - Passing test *files* used proxy tested functions. - Skipped test files contribute "untested breadth". - zero passing test files, `n_tested` set 1 avoid   divide--zero produce small non-zero denominator.

title: Package-Private Coverage Environment — cov_env
text: `cov_env` package-internal environment used test/coverage helpers (e.g., `cleanup_and_return_null()`) store temporary state without ever touching user's workspace (`.GlobalEnv`). created package load time binding `cov_env` exists *inside locked package namespace*, allows tests temporarily replace value (mock) still complying CRAN’s “.GlobalEnv modifications” rule.

title: Package-Private Coverage Environment — cov_env
Package-Private Coverage Environment — cov_env
text: **built?** package setup, `R/` files parsed evaluated part namespace loading. point line executed: “` cov_env <- new.env(parent = baseenv()) “` creates binding `cov_env` package namespace **** namespace locked R. namespace locked, add remove bindings—may change *values* existing bindings. pre-creating `cov_env`, tests can safely temporarily replace (e.g., `testthat::local_mocked_bindings()`), modify *existing* binding rather creating new one.  (See: R’s environment/namespace locking rules testthat’s guidance mocked bindings must already exist.)  [1](https://colinfay./writing-r-extensions/writing-r-documentation-files.html)[2](https://contributor.r-project.org/cran-cookbook/code_issues.html) **`parent = baseenv()`?** Using `baseenv()` isolates lookups fall user’s workspace. keeps internal state separate avoids accidental reads writes `.GlobalEnv`.  [3](https://github.com/coolbutuseless/CRAN-checks) **Testing tip:** unit tests, can temporarily replace binding without editing global environment: “` testthat::local_mocked_bindings(   .package = "yourpkg",   cov_env  = new.env(parent = baseenv()) ) “` works `cov_env` *binding already exists* namespace; `local_mocked_bindings()` create new bindings locked namespace.  [2](https://contributor.r-project.org/cran-cookbook/code_issues.html)

Packages appear `Depends` field **exported    symbols imported** supplied `env`. makes calls    `plot()`, `lm()`, `head()` resolve correctly inside environments whose    parent `.GlobalEnv`.
